title: Warehouse Ventilation Rate
summary: Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m²) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Minimum outdoor air ventilation rate for general warehouses

evidence_quote: Table 6-1 of Standard 62.1 recommends 0.3 L/(s·m²) of ventilation as a design criterion for warehouse ventilation, although this amount may be insufficient when stored materials have harmful emissions.

title: Bus Terminal Ventilation Concern
summary: Enclosed bus loading areas present serious health and safety problems due to emission of carbon monoxide (CO) by cars and oxides of nitrogen (NOx) by buses.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Primary ventilation concern in enclosed bus terminals

evidence_quote: Three major problems are encountered, the first and most serious of which is emission of carbon monoxide (CO) by cars and oxides of nitrogen (NOx) by buses, which can cause serious illness and possibly death.

title: Underground Garage CO Monitoring
summary: All underground garages should have facilities for testing the CO concentration or should have the garage checked periodically.
evidence_quote: All underground garages should have facilities for testing the CO concentration or should have the garage checked periodically.

title: Warehouse Load Characteristics
summary: In general, internal loads from lighting, people, and miscellaneous sources are low. Most of the load is thermal transmission and infiltration.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Primary load sources in general warehouses

evidence_quote: In general, internal loads from lighting, people, and miscellaneous sources are low. Most of the load is thermal transmission and infiltration.

title: Humid Climate Warehouse Humidity Control
summary: In humid climates, space humidity can become unacceptably high when cooling equipment is off due to infiltration of moist air.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Humidity control challenge in humid climates

evidence_quote: In humid climates, special attention should be given to the sensible and latent loads' variations for cases where the warehouse or distribution center is conditioned or cooled by thermostatically controlled packaged HVAC equipment.

title: Fume Hood Type Application
summary: Variable Volume fume hoods are for moderate to highly hazardous processes with varying procedures.
evidence_quote: Variable Volume (constant face velocity). ... Application: Moderate to highly hazardous processes; varying procedures.

title: Perchloric Acid Hood Requirement
summary: Perchloric acid exhaust systems must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system due to explosion hazard.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Mandatory safety feature for perchloric acid exhaust systems

evidence_quote: Perchloric Acid. ... To alleviate this hazard, the exhaust system must be equipped with an internal water washdown and drainage system, and the ductwork must be constructed of smooth, impervious, cleanable materials that are resistant to acid attack.

title: Cultural Heritage Zoning
summary: Activities that pose a potential threat to collections environments should be kept away from collections zones, with exhaust from these areas located away and downwind from any fresh air intakes.
evidence_quote: Activities that pose a potential threat to collections environments (food areas, loading docks, fabrication and rough shops, maintenance and housekeeping, etc.) should be kept away from collections zones, with exhaust from these areas located away and downwind from any fresh air intakes for collections systems and the rest of the building.

title: Vapor Pressure Differential Impact
summary: Diffusion of vapor, combined with interior wall temperatures, can cause significant structural damage ranging from mold growth to structural failure.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Risk of vapor diffusion through building envelope

evidence_quote: Diffusion in either direction, combined with interior wall temperatures, can cause significant structural damage ranging from mold growth in warm environments to structural failure in situations of repetitive freeze/thaw cycles.

title: Cultural Heritage Envelope Alternatives
summary: If envelope performance is questionable, consider alternatives like microenvironments, offsite storage, or box-in-box solutions.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Alternatives for containment of preservation environments

evidence_quote: If specific conditions are necessary for long-term preservation, alternatives for containment of preservation environments should be considered, such as Microenvironments (cabinetry, certain housing solutions, small-scale freezing); Offsite, purpose-built storage; Limited envelope upgrades in part of the structure, often called box-in-box solutions.

title: Cultural Heritage Latent Capacity
summary: Adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, leading to unsafe relative humidity conditions.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Importance of latent capacity in cultural heritage applications

evidence_quote: Though common when specifying some systems types such as four-pipe subcool/reheat and desiccant designs, adequate latent capacity is often disregarded with two-pipe and standard package unit designs, resulting in environments that may be able to maintain temperature control, but commonly see relative humidity conditions rise beyond safe limits as exterior temperatures climb and cooling capacity is dedicated to the sensible load.

title: Commissioning Start Timing
summary: Commissioning should begin during predesign, and formally continue through the first year of occupancy and operations.
evidence_quote: Commissioning should begin during predesign, and formally continue through the first year of occupancy and operations.

title: Acoustic Design Criteria
summary: The Noise Criteria (NC) method is the most commonly used for HVAC system noise design.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Primary acoustic design method for HVAC systems

evidence_quote: The following suggested design procedure uses the Noise Criteria (NC) method, which is the most commonly used.

title: Air Curtain Effectiveness Range
summary: Air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on the type and application.
evidence_quote: Research (e.g., Pappas and Tassou [2003]) shows that air curtains have a range of effectiveness from 60 to 90%, depending on the type and application.

title: Air Curtain Sizing Requirement
summary: To maximize effectiveness, the air curtain unit must at least cover or slightly overlap the entire opening, and have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Air curtain sizing and velocity requirement

evidence_quote: To maximize effectiveness, the air curtain unit must at least cover or slightly overlap the entire opening, and have a minimum velocity projection of 2 m/s at the target surface (Wang and Zhong 2014).
